title: B.Sc. (Hons with Research) in Climate Change and Sustainability
content: This is a four year, rigorous and research-oriented undergraduate degree program designed to build a strong foundation in Climate Change and Sustainability. This interdisciplinary degree equips students to understand and respond to the global climate crisis by integrating knowledge from earth sciences, environmental policy, sustainability practices, and green technologies. It promotes systems thinking, ecological awareness, and sustainability leadership, preparing graduates for careers in climate advocacy, environmental management, and international development. Students will pursue sixth month’s project in different R&D laboratories of national repute. Students will provide a platform to publish research papers under the guidance of faculty members at 4th year. This will enhance the skills of students towards future research and other prospectives. 10+2 with Science, with a minimum aggregate of 50% marks. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057) score will be given preference in the merit list. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 02 03 04 01 Graduates will be able to analyze the scientific, social, and economic dimensions of climate change, including its causes, impacts, and mitigation strategies, to devise sustainable solutions for environmental challenges. 02 Students will gain hands-on experience through fieldwork, internships, and projects, enabling them to implement climate adaptation and mitigation strategies, such as renewable energy solutions, environmental impact assessments, and geospatial analytics. 03 Graduates will be equipped to engage in policy development, environmental advocacy, and stakeholder collaboration, fostering sustainable practices in government, NGOs, or corporate sectors to address global climate and sustainability challenges. 04
